notes: This file is owned by Agent 05 only.
Structure nests rounds inside sprints to keep history organized.

sprint_meta.notes: WK53: Terrain elevation visual sprint. Agent 05 Wave 0 consult-only: gameplay implications advisory for future elevation gameplay systems.

wk53_r1_sky_fog_architecture.what_i_changed: Consult-only work. Created gameplay implications advisory doc at .cursor/plans/wk53_gameplay_elevation_advisory.md covering building placement on slopes, movement cost scaling, combat elevation advantages, resource economy impact, and heightmap API requirements for future sprite implementation.
wk53_r1_sky_fog_architecture.advisory_summary: Elevation is visual-only in WK53 but data model must support 4 gameplay systems later: (1) Building placement restrictions (slope ≤10°, Guardhouse ≤15°); (2) Movement costs (+20% per 10° uphill, cliffs ≥45° impassable); (3) Combat bonuses (+15% damage on high ground, +1 tile ranged range); (4) Economy tuning (farms on slopes > 5° get -10% gold penalty). Provided 7 API functions required for Agent 03 heightmap design: get_terrain_height, get_slope_at_point, get_slope_between_points, get_max_slope_at_footprint, is_cliff, get_elevation_tier, get_terrain_cost_multiplier. All features marked 'future sprint implementation' — no gameplay code touched WK53.
files_reviewed: config.py (building types, costs, sizes, enemy stats, hero stats)

evidence.advisory_doc: Written to .cursor/plans/wk53_gameplay_elevation_advisory.md with 5 sections (building placement, movement, combat, resources, API), config constants table, risks/mitigations, and 7 public API functions for heightmap system.

coordination_notes: Agent 03: Use the 7 API functions as contract for Wave 0 architecture doc. Heightmap must support slope queries, elevation tiers, and terrain cost multipliers.
Future implementation (Agent 05 in WK54+): Building placement gates, movement cost scaling, combat bonuses, economy penalties all depend on this API being complete and accurate.

sprint_meta.notes: WK57: Underground vertical stacking. Agent 05 owns Wave 4 (layer pathfinding + underground fog) and Wave 5 (hero transitions + spawning + AI cave exploration).

what_i_changed: game/systems/pathfinding.py: Added LayerPathfinder class wrapping existing find_path() with layer-aware routing. Handles 4 cases: surface-only, underground-only, surface-to-underground (via cave entrance), underground-to-surface. Underground A* uses 4-directional movement on area walkability grid with local/world coord conversion.

game/world.py: Added underground_visibility dict in World.__init__. Added init_underground_fog(area) to create per-area UNSEEN grids. Added reveal_underground_circle(area_id, local_x, local_z, radius) for circular fog reveal.
game/sim_engine.py: After underground area generation in setup_initial_state(), added fog grid initialization loop and LayerPathfinder instantiation (self.layer_pathfinder).

tests/test_underground.py (new): 13 tests covering UndergroundArea data model (chambers, walkability, heightmap, corridors), underground fog (init, reveal, bounds, unknown area), and LayerPathfinder (surface-only, start==goal, unknown goal, underground-only).
follow_ups: Wave 5 (Agent 05): hero descent/ascent state machine, cave entry via POI interaction, underground enemy spawning, AI cave exploration scoring.

what_i_changed: game/entities/hero.py: Added underground_area_id, underground_local_x, underground_local_z fields to Hero.__init__. Added begin_descent(area_id, entrance_x, entrance_y) and begin_ascent() transition methods.

game/systems/poi_interaction.py: Added _underground_areas and _sim_engine references to POIInteractionSystem.__init__. Replaced _handle_dungeon from flavor-text-only to full cave entry: triggers hero.begin_descent(), marks entrance chamber explored, reveals underground fog, spawns underground enemies. Added module-level _spawn_underground_enemies() function that instantiates enemies from chamber data at layer=-1.

game/sim_engine.py: Wired underground_areas and sim self-reference to poi_interaction_system after setup. Added WK57 Wave 5E retreat check in update loop: calls check_underground_hero_retreat for underground heroes each tick.
game/underground.py: Added check_underground_hero_retreat(hero, underground_areas) function that triggers begin_ascent() when hero HP < 30% max or all chambers cleared.

ai/behaviors/poi_awareness.py: Added dungeon_factor to score_poi_for_personality(): aggressive/brave heroes get 1.5x, cautious heroes get 0.2x (low level) or 0.8x, low HP heroes (<70%) get 0.1x multiplier. Updated maybe_visit_poi() to target entrance tile directly for dungeon POIs.

tests/test_underground.py: Extended from 13 to 25 tests. Added TestHeroTransitions (5 tests: descent/ascent layer, local coords, field existence), TestHeroRetreat (5 tests: low HP, healthy, all cleared, surface no-op, missing area), TestDungeonScoring (2 tests: aggressive bonus, low HP avoidance).

method_check.command: python -c "from game.entities.hero import Hero; h = Hero.__new__(Hero); print(hasattr(h, 'begin_descent'), hasattr(h, 'begin_ascent'))"

sprint_meta.notes: WK60: Make It Fun sprint. Agent 05 owns 7 features: wave events, lair tuning, guild hero cap, hero cost doubling, guardhouse arrows, difficulty system, dev mode.

round_meta.build_target: 7 gameplay features for Make It Fun v1.5.9

scope_guardrails_followed: Did not edit: game/ui/ (Agent 08), ai/basic_ai.py (Agent 06), game/audio/ (Agent 14).
Edited game/engine.py and game/sim_engine.py only for wiring (guild cap check, guardhouse enemies param, difficulty/wave system init) — minimal changes per Feature 3/5/8 wiring.
what_i_changed: FEATURE 1 — WAVE EVENTS: Created game/systems/wave_events.py with WaveEventSystem. 8-event wave table (Goblin Raid at 3:00 through Demon Siege at 25:00), cycling with escalating count multipliers after table exhausts. Emits 'wave_incoming' and 'wave_cleared' EventBus events for Agent 08 HUD. Awards bonus gold on wave clear. Difficulty-aware (scales enemy counts, HP, damage). Respects MAX_ALIVE_ENEMIES * 1.5 overflow cap.

FEATURE 1 — SPAWNER TUNING: Updated game/systems/spawner.py: mixed enemy types (weighted pools by game time: early/mid/late), enemies_per_wave cap raised 4->6, spawn interval floor 21000->15000ms, interval reduction -250->-500ms per escalation. Difficulty multipliers applied to spawn interval and enemy stats on creation.

FEATURE 2 — LAIR TUNING: Updated game/entities/lair.py: GoblinCamp 8.5->6.0s (50%x2/30%x3/20%x1), WolfDen 7.5->5.5s (60%x2/40%x3), SkeletonCrypt 10.5->8.0s (60%x2skel/20%x2archer/20%x1each), SpiderNest 7.0->5.0s (50%x3/30%x4/20%x2), BanditCamp 10.0->7.5s (60%x2/30%x1/10%x3). Updated config.py: MAX_ALIVE_ENEMIES 32->48, LAIR_INITIAL_COUNT 4->5, LAIR_MIN_DISTANCE_FROM_CASTLE_TILES 18->15. Updated game/systems/lairs.py: difficulty multipliers applied to lair spawn intervals (via effective_dt scaling) and enemy HP/damage on spawn.

FEATURE 3 — GUILD HERO CAP: Updated game/entities/buildings/hiring_mixin.py: added max_heroes attribute (default GUILD_MAX_HEROES=8 from config), can_hire() returns False when heroes_hired >= max_heroes, added on_hero_death() to decrement counter. Updated game/engine.py try_hire_hero(): checks guild.can_hire() with 'Guild is full!' feedback. Updated game/engine.py _cleanup_after_combat(): detects newly dead heroes and calls home_building.on_hero_death() to free guild slots.
FEATURE 4 — HERO COST: Updated config.py HeroConfig.hire_cost: 50->100.

FEATURE 5 — GUARDHOUSE ARROWS: Updated game/entities/buildings/defensive.py Guardhouse: added ranged attack (8-tile range, 12 damage, 2s cooldown) using the same projectile event pattern as BallistaTower. Fires arrows at nearest enemy within range while also spawning guards as before. Updated game/engine.py and game/sim_engine.py: pass enemies list to guardhouse update call.

FEATURE 8 — DIFFICULTY SYSTEM: Created game/systems/difficulty.py with DifficultyLevel enum (EASY/NORMAL/HARD) and DifficultySystem class. Multiplier tables from DifficultyConfig in config.py. get_multiplier(key) for spawn_interval, enemies_per_wave, enemy_hp, enemy_damage, wave_event_count. set_difficulty(level) with lock support. Wired into spawner.py, lairs.py, wave_events.py. Dev mode defaults to Easy.

FEATURE 9 — DEV MODE: Added DEV_MODE check at bottom of config.py (KINGDOM_DEV_MODE env var). Overrides: HERO_HIRE_COST=0, STARTING_GOLD=999999, GUILD_MAX_HEROES=999. Console banner on activation. DifficultySystem defaults to Easy when DEV_MODE active.
Updated game/systems/__init__.py: exports DifficultySystem, DifficultyLevel, WaveEventSystem.
Updated game/sim_engine.py: creates DifficultySystem instance, passes to EnemySpawner, LairSystem, WaveEventSystem. Calls wave_event_system.update() in tick loop.
Updated tests/test_spawner.py: adjusted enemies_per_wave cap assertion from 4 to 6.
how_to_test: $env:KINGDOM_DEV_MODE='1'; python main.py  # verify dev mode banner and free heroes
follow_ups: Agent 08 (R2): Wave warning HUD toasts, guild 'N/8' display, 'Guild is full!' HUD feedback, difficulty menu UI (Easy/Normal/Hard selector + Lock Difficulty button), '[DEV MODE]' HUD label.
Agent 14 (R2): Horn SFX for wave warnings, wave-clear fanfare.
Agent 10 (R3): Perf check with 48 enemy cap + wave events active.
Phase 2 playtest tuning: all values in config.py are starting points — will iterate on spawn rates, gold costs, wave timing, difficulty curve.

sprint_meta.notes: WK61: Playtest Polish sprint. Agent 05 owns 6 items: hero HP -40%, market time halved, guardhouse 2 arrows, guild/inn healing boost, enemy building AI priority, building destruction + rubble system.

WK61-R4-BUG-006: Guardhouse sets GUARDHOUSE_MAX_HP=250 with explicit hp/max_hp; Building.get_hp_state() exposes HP for UI/renderer.

follow_ups: Agent 06: Continue R4-BUG-005 AI-side idle regression (exploration/bounty gating in ai/).

what_i_changed: WK61-R6-BUG-001 (data): Added has_tax_stash_data / get_overlay_tax_gold contract on Building (None = no stash) and TaxStashMixin (int, 0 when empty).
NeutralBuilding now inherits TaxStashMixin for consistent stored_tax_gold + collect_taxes.
config.py: TAX_STASH_BUILDING_TYPES and NON_TAX_STASH_BUILDING_TYPES document taxable vs non-taxable player building types.

what_i_changed: WK61-R8-BUG-001: Hero.buy_item now resolves tax deposit shop via _shop_for_tax_deposit — uses inside_building when set, else pending_task_building when pending_task is shopping (WK11 deferred purchase runs after pop_out).
game/entities/hero.py: optional shop_building kwarg on buy_item for explicit shop routing.
tests/test_wk61_r8_marketplace_potion_tax.py: regression via real Marketplace + do_shopping deferred path and direct buy_item after pop-out.

evidence.root_cause: Deferred shopping finalizes purchase after pop_out_of_building clears inside_building; tax was only stashed when inside_building was set.

follow_ups: Agent 11: Verify R8 gates and targeted marketplace potion tax regression after Agent 05 fix.

what_i_changed: WK61-R10-FEAT-001: config.py NEUTRAL_TAX_PER_MINUTE house 9 / farm 12 / food_stand 10; wired neutral_buildings.py constructors; ECONOMY_TAX_RATE_MULT=1.4 documented (shop path unchanged for R8).

WK61-R10-FEAT-002: Moved spawner timings to config (initial_no_spawn_ms 1500, first_wave_interval_ms 3500, extra_spawn_delay_ms 5000, GOBLIN*2+extra); wave_events.py derives schedule from WAVE_EVENT first_event_minute 2.0 / interval 1.75.
WK61-R10-FEAT-003: Hero hunger sim contract — next_meal_due_ms, hunger_urgent, buy_meal_at_food_stand() with add_tax_gold + hero_ate event; HUNGER_INTERVAL_MS 180000, FOOD_MEAL_COST_GOLD 10.
tests/test_wk61_r10_economy_tax_accrual.py and tests/test_wk61_r10_wave_timing.py added.

what_i_changed: WK61-R11-BUG-001: game/systems/wave_events.py _reserve_wave_spawn_slots trims oldest alive enemies when wave_cap full so Wolf Pack composition (Wolf class) is not dropped; tests/test_wk61_r11_wolf_pack_spawn.py headless Wolf Pack spawn + cap regression.

sprint_meta.notes: WK61 playtest 2: fix 6 regressions/requests found in Jaimie's second playtest.

summary_bullets: ITEM 1 (CRITICAL) — Hero idle regression: Buildings now have is_alive (WK61-BUG-003), causing hasattr checks in bounty_pursuit.py and basic_ai.py to incorrectly treat building/lair targets as enemy targets. Fixed by excluding building_type targets from zone-distance chase abort (bounty_pursuit.py L558) and from range-to-fight checks (L571), and by ejecting non-lair building targets from FIGHTING state (basic_ai.py L364).
ITEM 2 — Guard HP halved: guard.py max_hp reduced from 80 to 40.
ITEM 3 — Buildings attackable: Enemy near-town other_bias loosened from 0.4 to 0.7 so guilds/marketplace/blacksmith are attacked when enemies are nearby (enemy.py find_target).
ITEM 4 — Guardhouse arrow offsets: Arrow origin spread increased from +/-4px to +/-12px horizontal and +/-4px vertical for visually distinct dual arrows (defensive.py).
ITEM 5 — Enemy ambient sounds wired: AudioSystem.update_enemy_ambient(enemies) now called each frame from _flush_event_bus in engine.py.

ITEM 6 — Building destruction cleanup: Added _cleanup_destroyed_buildings() to SimEngine.update() — was missing after architecture refactor. Buildings at 0 HP now get removed from sim.buildings, references cleared, rubble spawned, and BUILDING_DESTROYED events emitted. Also ported guild hero-death tracking to SimEngine cleanup path.

qa_smoke.summary: qa_smoke.py --quick passes, observe_sync shows heroes patrolling/fighting/shopping normally

sprint_meta.notes: WK64 AI Contracts & System Runner. Agent 05 owns Wave 1B: widen SystemContext + ordered SystemRunner.

wk64_pb_w1_runner.prompt_text: PHASE B Wave 1B (Gameplay). (1) equivalence audit of each system update(ctx,dt) vs bespoke call in SimEngine.update(); (2) widen SystemContext with 7 defaulted fields; (3) populate them in _build_system_context(); (4) create game/sim/system_runner.py (SystemRunner); (5) construct it with ONLY proven-equivalent systems (buff + wave_event) preserving exact execution order. Verify suite + determinism + qa_smoke. DO NOT COMMIT.

what_i_changed.summary: Widened SystemContext with 7 defaulted fields, populated them in _build_system_context(), created SystemRunner, and constructed it in SimEngine holding only the two proven-equivalent fire-and-forget systems (buff_system, wave_event_system). Kept their direct call sites in update() because they are NOT adjacent (other side-effecting logic runs between buff@668 and wave_event@720); collapsing would reorder gameplay. Preserved exact execution order -> determinism guard PASS.
files_touched: game/systems/protocol.py (added 7 defaulted fields to SystemContext + dataclasses.field import)
game/sim_engine.py (import SystemRunner; construct self._ordered_systems in __init__ after poi_interaction_system; populate 7 new ctx fields in _build_system_context())

equivalence_audit_table.action: MIGRATE -> in SystemRunner. Pure update(ctx,dt); reads ctx.heroes/buildings, prunes+applies aura buffs; no surrounding post-processing.

equivalence_audit_table.current_call: self.wave_event_system.update(system_ctx, dt) @ sim_engine.py:720 (after spawn-cap block, before separation)
equivalence_audit_table.action: MIGRATE -> in SystemRunner. Pure update(ctx,dt); mutates ctx.enemies/economy, emits events; no surrounding post-processing.

equivalence_audit_table.current_call: self.combat_system.update(system_ctx, dt) @ sim_engine.py:726, IMMEDIATELY followed by enemy_ranged_events emit_batch (727-728), get_emitted_events() (729), _route_combat_events() (730)
equivalence_audit_table.action: EXCEPTION (keep bespoke). update() is real work but _emitted_events MUST be drained + routed right after; NOT fire-and-forget.

equivalence_audit_table.current_call: self.nature_system.tick(dt, self.trees, world=, buildings=) @ sim_engine.py:624, wrapped in tree-tile bookkeeping (pre_keys 621 / new_keys tile-set 630-644)
equivalence_audit_table.action: EXCEPTION (keep bespoke). No update(ctx,dt); only tick(dt, trees, *, world, buildings) + surrounding world-tile sync.

equivalence_audit_table.current_call: new=self.spawner.spawn(dt); self.enemies.extend(new[:remaining_slots]) @ sim_engine.py:709-711 inside MAX_ALIVE_ENEMIES slot-cap block (705-717)
equivalence_audit_table.action: EXCEPTION (keep bespoke). update() (126-130) does UNCONDITIONAL ctx.enemies.extend(spawned); bypasses the slot cap -> would over-spawn.

equivalence_audit_table.current_call: lair=self.lair_system.spawn_enemies(dt, self.buildings); self.enemies.extend(lair[:remaining_slots]) @ sim_engine.py:715-717 inside same slot-cap block
equivalence_audit_table.action: EXCEPTION (keep bespoke). update() (116-120) does UNCONDITIONAL ctx.enemies.extend(spawned); bypasses the slot cap.

equivalence_audit_table.current_call: claimed=self.bounty_system.check_claims(self.heroes) -> HUD msgs + BOUNTY_CLAIMED emit_batch (755-770); then self.bounty_system.cleanup() (771)
equivalence_audit_table.action: EXCEPTION (keep bespoke). update() is a NO-OP (bounty.py:210-212, '_ = (ctx, dt)'). Real work is check_claims()+cleanup() w/ HUD side effects; runner would silently drop claim processing.

equivalence_audit_table.current_call: self.neutral_building_system.tick(dt, self.buildings, self.heroes, self.peasants, castle) @ sim_engine.py:774; castle=game_state.get('castle') (669)
equivalence_audit_table.action: EXCEPTION (keep bespoke). update() (177-180) derives castle via _find_castle(ctx.buildings); bespoke uses game_state['castle']. Castle source differs -> not provably identical.

equivalence_audit_table.current_call: self.poi_interaction_system.tick_cooldowns(pois, dt) (794) + check_interactions(heroes, pois, world, economy, event_bus, dt) (795-797)
equivalence_audit_table.action: EXCEPTION (keep bespoke). No update(ctx,dt); two separate bespoke methods.

wk64_pb_w1_runner.equivalence_conclusion: Only buff_system and wave_event_system are safe to drive through the runner: both are pure update(ctx,dt) with no surrounding post-processing. All 6 others are documented exceptions (combat: event routing; spawner/lair: slot-cap bypass; bounty: no-op update + HUD side effects in check_claims/cleanup; neutral_buildings: differing castle source; nature: no update(ctx,dt) + tile bookkeeping; poi: two bespoke methods). Matches the plan-predicted conclusion.

system_runner_membership.order: buff_system first, then wave_event_system (their current relative order in update())
system_runner_membership.why_safe: Both implement update(ctx, dt) as pure fire-and-forget: they read from ctx and dt, mutate only ctx.enemies/ctx.economy and emit events, and have ZERO surrounding post-processing at their call sites. Their bespoke calls are already literally self.X.update(system_ctx, dt).

call_sites_collapsed.reason: Per the plan Decision rule (preserve exact execution order at all costs), buff_system.update (sim_engine.py:668) and wave_event_system.update (sim_engine.py:720) are NOT adjacent: peasant spawning, enemy/guard updates, and the spawner/lair slot-cap block run between them. Collapsing into a single update_all would force them back-to-back, reordering side-effecting work and risking determinism/gameplay regressions. Kept the two direct calls; SystemRunner is constructed and seeded for future adjacency-driven consolidation. Non-negotiable deliverables (widened+populated SystemContext, SystemRunner class constructed, equivalence table) are all met.

commands_run.result: PASS: no violations found (proves no side-effect reorder)

evidence.headless_ctx_spotcheck: peasants=list(0), guards=list(0), bounties=list(0), pois=list(32), rubble_records=list(0), lairs=list(5), castle=BuildingType.CASTLE; runner systems=[BuffSystem, WaveEventSystem]

recommended_next_actions: Gate 1 (Agent 11): re-verify full suite + determinism + qa_smoke and grep-confirm SystemContext fields + SystemRunner wiring.
Future sprint: if buff/wave_event are ever made adjacent (or a no-op-between proof established), collapse their two call sites into one _ordered_systems.update_all(ctx, dt).
Future sprint: revisit spawner/lair migration only if the MAX_ALIVE_ENEMIES slot-cap moves into the systems; revisit neutral_buildings once castle source is unified to ctx.castle.

sprint_meta.notes: WK65 Round 0 (De-Slop Foundation). Agent 05 Wave 1: write buildings/systems characterization net GREEN on current code. Wave-2 poi_interaction deletion is a later activation.

round_meta.wave: Wave 1 (characterization pins, green on current code)

wk65_w1_characterization.prompt_text: Wave-1 ONLY: create tests/test_wk65_buildings_systems_characterization.py, GREEN on current unmodified code. Pin (1) ranged-tower fire cadence via SINGULAR _last_ranged_event + cooldown (do NOT assert plural _last_ranged_events — deferred to Round C), (2) building update() dispatch outcomes, (3) DifficultySystem hp/damage scaling at a couple levels. CREATE only that one test file; edit no production code. DO NOT COMMIT. DO NOT PUSH.

what_i_changed.summary: Created the buildings/systems characterization safety net (14 tests) that pins observable behavior Round C will dedup. No production code touched. Green before any deletion exists; this is the bracket proving the Wave-2 deletions are inert.
what_i_changed.production_code_edited: NONE (test file only, per file-ownership table)

ranged_tower_fire_cadence: Guardhouse.update(dt, guards_list, enemies): fires at in-range enemy, deals GUARDHOUSE_ARROW_DAMAGE * GUARDHOUSE_ARROWS_PER_SHOT (12*2=24) per volley, sets SINGULAR _last_ranged_event (type=ranged_projectile, kind=arrow, to_x/to_y=enemy pos), latches target.
Guardhouse respects GUARDHOUSE_ARROW_COOLDOWN (2.0s): no second volley on a sub-cooldown tick; fires again after cooldown elapses.
Guardhouse clears _last_ranged_event and target when cooldown ready but no enemy in range.
BallistaTower.update(dt, enemies): deals attack_damage (15), sets singular _last_ranged_event, respects attack_interval (2.0s) cooldown.
Unconstructed ranged tower (is_constructed=False) performs no attack (pins the early-return guard).
DELIBERATELY did NOT assert on _last_ranged_events (plural) — deferred to Round C RangedAttackMixin per plan.
building_update_dispatch: Guardhouse.update returns True (should_spawn) once spawn_interval (30s) elapses and guard cap not reached; False under interval and False at/over max_guards cap.
TradingPost.update(dt, economy): grants income_amount gold once income_interval elapses; tracks total_income_generated.
Marketplace.update(dt, economy): accrues passive tax into stored_tax_gold once MARKETPLACE_PASSIVE_TAX_INTERVAL_MS elapses (deterministic via get_rng; sim clock pinned via monkeypatch).
Marketplace timed-research: research_progress climbs 0 -> mid -> 1.0 across research_duration_ms; advance_research completes potions research.
difficulty_scaling: Multiplier table: NORMAL neutral (1.0), EASY softens hp/damage (<1.0), HARD hardens (>1.0); unknown keys fall back to 1.0.
NORMAL leaves a fresh Goblin unscaled.
EASY scales a fresh Goblin hp/damage DOWN via exact production formula max(1,int(round(base*mult))) applied only when mult!=1.0.
HARD scales a fresh Skeleton hp/damage UP via the same formula; verified strict increase.
Pin mirrors the formula triplicated across spawner.py:189-197, lairs.py:144-153, wave_events.py:254-259 that Round C unifies into DifficultySystem.apply_to_enemy.

evidence.pytest_result: 14 passed, 1 warning in ~0.1s (stable across two runs)

surprising_findings_for_round_c: Guardhouse already maintains BOTH the singular _last_ranged_event AND the plural _last_ranged_events list; the singular is literally set to _last_ranged_events[0]. The plural exists only on Guardhouse (multi-arrow), not on BallistaTower or Enemy.do_attack — so a Round-C RangedAttackMixin must treat plural as a Guardhouse-specific extension, not a base contract.

BallistaTower.update probes self.get_ranged_spec() via hasattr+try/except but the real BallistaTower defines no get_ranged_spec, so it ALWAYS falls to defaults (kind=bolt, color=(180,180,200), size=2). This is the get_ranged_spec entanglement the plan deferred — the only real implementer is the test double in conftest. Round C should decide whether buildings get a real get_ranged_spec or the defaults become explicit.

The hp/damage scaling is byte-identical in all three sites (spawner/lairs/wave_events) including the mult!=1.0 short-circuit and the max(1, round) rounding; a single DifficultySystem.apply_to_enemy(enemy) is a clean drop-in. Note the guard does NOT scale speed/xp/gold — only hp and attack_power — so apply_to_enemy must preserve that exact scope.

Guardhouse.update has dual responsibility (guard spawn + ranged) and returns a guard-spawn bool while mutating ranged state as a side effect; the two concerns share one tick path. This is the stringly-adjacent dispatch the plan flags for Round B/C.
